steps.title: Block the body (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a rounded rectangle for the can's body — a little taller than wide, with softly curved corners. This is the main reservoir.

steps.title: Plan the spout and handle (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a long diagonal line angling up and out from the upper-left side — that's the spout's centerline. On the right, sketch a loose arc loop for the handle that rises up and curves back down to the body.

steps.title: Add the spout shape (pencil)
steps.description: Around your spout centerline, sketch two roughly parallel lines tapering slightly toward the tip. At the far end, add a small flared oval — the rose where the water sprinkles out.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the body and spout
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Lay down a confident line around the body silhouette, continuing out along the spout to the rose. Where the spout meets the body, land the lines right at the junction — don't let them float past or stop short.

steps.title: Ink the handle (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the handle as two parallel arcs from the top of the body, over, and back down to the side. A heavier press where the handle joins the can helps anchor those junctions.

steps.title: Add the rose detail (ink)
steps.description: On the flared end of the spout, add a small ellipse for the front face of the rose and a few dots inside to suggest the sprinkler holes. A few marks suggest more than rendering every hole.
